findings.title: Real-Time Co-Editing Feature Gap
findings.description: CollabDocs currently lacks real-time co-editing capabilities, which is a critical feature offered by Competitor X (LiveWrite) and Competitor Y (CoEdit Pro). This gap poses a risk of losing enterprise customers who are actively seeking this functionality.
assumptions: Enterprise customers prioritize real-time collaboration features.
Competitors are effectively marketing their real-time editing capabilities.

evidence.excerpt: Competitor launched real-time co-editing — we need parity.
Enterprise customers asking for simultaneous editing.

recommendations: Prioritize development of real-time co-editing features to achieve parity with competitors.

findings.title: Competitive Positioning of Competitors
findings.description: Competitor X focuses on consumer-friendly features with no additional costs for real-time editing, while Competitor Y targets enterprise needs with admin controls and audit trails. This indicates a bifurcation in the market where CollabDocs can position itself as a hybrid solution that combines workflow governance with real-time collaboration.
assumptions: There is a market for hybrid solutions that cater to both enterprise and consumer needs.

evidence.excerpt: Competitor Y targets regulated industries that need audit trails and admin controls.

recommendations: Position CollabDocs as a hybrid solution that integrates real-time collaboration with robust document workflow features.

findings.title: Risk of Customer Churn Due to Feature Gaps
findings.description: Three enterprise accounts have formally requested simultaneous editing features, citing competitors' recent launches as the trigger. The combined ARR at risk is $420K, indicating a significant threat to revenue if these features are not implemented promptly.
assumptions: Customers will churn if their feature requests are not met within a reasonable timeframe.

evidence.excerpt: Three enterprise accounts have submitted formal feature requests for simultaneous multi-user editing.

recommendations: Accelerate the development timeline for real-time co-editing features to mitigate churn risk.

findings.title: Explore Conflict Resolution Strategies
findings.description: As CollabDocs moves towards real-time co-editing, a formal conflict resolution strategy is essential. Evaluating both Operational Transformation (OT) and Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (CRDT) will help determine the best fit for our document model and user experience.
assumptions: A well-defined conflict resolution strategy will enhance user experience and reduce data loss.

evidence.excerpt: A formal conflict resolution strategy is required as we move toward real-time co-editing.

recommendations: Conduct a thorough evaluation of OT vs. CRDT to inform the development of conflict resolution features.

summary: CollabDocs faces critical gaps in real-time co-editing capabilities compared to competitors, which poses a risk of customer churn and revenue loss. Positioning as a hybrid solution that integrates real-time collaboration with existing workflow features could provide a strategic advantage. Immediate action is required to develop these features to maintain competitive positioning.
